core.when: When implementing TimeSeriesDataRecorder subclass

core.when: When configuring SQLite storage for data persistence

consequence.description: SQLite will throw 'SQLite objects created in a thread can only be used in that same thread' errors when recorders run concurrently, blocking data persistence

core.when: When fetching data from external providers

core.when: When implementing position sizing defaults
core.action: Apply tiered position sizing: 20% when no positions, 50% when <=10 positions, 100% otherwise

consequence.description: Improper position sizing violates the Chinese market regulatory guidelines encoded in the defaults, leading to over-concentration and potential significant losses

core.when: When using yfinance or similar external data providers
core.action: Kdata from polling-based providers MUST NOT be claimed as real-time data

consequence.description: Claiming polling-based data as real-time misleads users about data freshness and violates data provider terms of service

_classification.rationale: A.6 FAILS: repo token "repo_token:yfinance" in when/action

core.when: When implementing custom data schemas that inherit from Mixin
core.action: Include a 'timestamp' column of type DateTime representing the event happen time — this column is automatically indexed in composite indexes (timestamp, entity_id) and (timestamp, code) by the register machinery

consequence.description: Custom schemas that omit or rename the timestamp column break all Factor operations, query_data filters, and the composite index strategy in register.py:50-64, causing silent data retrieval failures across the entire system

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "Mixin", "DateTime" in when/action

core.when: When encoding entity identifiers for any tradable entity
core.action: Format entity_id as {entity_type}_{exchange}_{code} (e.g., stock_sh_600000, stockus_nasdaq_AAPL) — code may itself contain underscores since split() takes index [0] as entity_type, index [1] as exchange, and joins each remaining parts as code

consequence.description: Incorrectly formatted entity_id causes decode_entity_id() to return wrong (entity_type, exchange, code) tuples, corrupting all downstream entity lookups, trading signals, and factor operations that rely on decode_entity_id

_classification.rationale: A.4 FAILS: project-specific snake_case "entity_id, entity_type, entity_type" in when/action

core.when: When defining OHLCV kdata schema subclasses
core.action: Name the schema class using the convention {EntitySchema}{IntervalLevel}{AdjustType}Kdata (e.g., Stock1dKdata, Stock1dHfqKdata, Index1hQfqKdata) and register it with the matching entity_type and db_name

consequence.description: Non-compliant naming breaks the {entity_schema}{level}{adjust_type}Kdata pattern expected by get_kdata_schema() lookups and all record_data/query_data operations for that schema

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "EntitySchema", "IntervalLevel", "AdjustType" in when/action

core.when: When using IntervalLevel comparisons for trading level ordering or threshold checks
core.action: Treat IntervalLevel ordering as based on millisecond duration: 1m < 5m < 15m < 30m < 1h < 4h < 1d < 1wk < 1mon — comparisons use __ge__/__lt__ which call to_ms() on both operands

consequence.description: Using string-value comparison instead of the built-in ordering produces wrong level hierarchy — for example, '1h' string is less than '1d' by string comparison but correctly greater by ms-based ordering; this breaks level filtering and trading loop traversal

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "IntervalLevel" in when/action

core.when: When implementing or interpreting Factor result_df filter_result values
core.action: Apply this semantics to filter_result column: True = BUY signal, False = SELL signal, None/NaN = NO ACTION — these values are consumed by TargetSelector and drawer_annotation_df for trade annotations

consequence.description: Misinterpreting filter_result polarity causes all trading decisions to be inverted — BUY signals trigger sells and SELL signals trigger buys, producing the opposite of intended portfolio behavior

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "Factor", "TargetSelector", "NaN" in when/action

core.when: When building factor DataFrames for use with Factor or ScoreFactor
core.action: Set MultiIndex levels as level[0]=entity_id, level[1]=timestamp — each factor operations (transform, accumulate, score, filter) expect this index structure; category_field and time_field parameters default to 'entity_id' and 'timestamp' respectively

consequence.description: Incorrect MultiIndex ordering causes factor operations to fail with index alignment errors or produce wrong entity-timestamp groupings, silently corrupting trading signals

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "ScoreFactor", "DataFrames", "Factor" in when/action

core.when: When using the TRADITIONAL backtesting Trader class (not the REST API server)
core.action: Use AdjustType enum values exactly as qfq (前复权/pre-adjust), hfq (后复权/post-adjust), bfq (不复权/no adjustment) — never pass raw strings or adjust types outside this enum to trading or factor operations

consequence.description: Passing non-enum adjust types causes AdjustType comparisons to fail silently, resulting in unadjusted prices being used when adjusted prices were intended, or vice versa, corrupting all factor calculations and trading signal generation

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "Trader", "AdjustType" in when/action

core.when: When setting up multi-level trading loops in the Trader class
core.action: Traverse trading levels in ascending order (shortest interval first) as defined by IntervalLevel to_ms() values — signals from higher levels must be cached and promoted to the current cycle, not recalculated per level independently

consequence.description: Processing levels out of order causes signals from higher-frequency levels to be unavailable at the lower-frequency execution cycle, breaking the multi-level signal aggregation architecture and producing incomplete or incorrect trading decisions

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "Trader", "IntervalLevel" in when/action

core.when: When running the Dash & Plotly UI (zvt command) for live or interactive trading decisions
core.action: Claim that the Dash & Plotly UI supports real-time market data processing or user-interactive trading — this UI mode is explicitly for backtest and research only

consequence.description: Users rely on the UI to execute live trades based on factors and signals, but the Dash UI does not connect to any live data feed or broker API, resulting in simulated-only display with no actual order execution capability

_classification.rationale: A.6 FAILS: repo token "repo_token:zvt" in when/action

core.when: When presenting or reporting the TRADITIONAL Trader backtested returns to users or stakeholders
core.action: Claim that backtested returns equal expected live trading returns — backtesting ignores market impact, financing costs, execution delays, slippage model limitations, and broker fees that differ from the simulated default of 0.1% buy_cost + 0.1% sell_cost + 0.1% slippage

consequence.description: Users make live capital allocation decisions based on inflated backtest returns that exclude real-world execution costs and market friction, leading to severe underperformance when deployed live and potential financial loss

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "Trader" in when/action

core.when: When using the TRADITIONAL Trader class or its subclasses for live trading execution
core.action: Claim or imply that the Trader class supports real-time trading execution with broker integration — the traditional Trader only supports backtesting via simulated account; live trading requires the separate zvt_server REST API combined with a broker integration (e.g., QMT) that is not included in this codebase

consequence.description: Users execute strategies expecting live broker execution, but the Trader class only runs simulated backtests with no actual order routing, causing real financial exposure without any position management or risk controls

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "Trader" in when/action

core.when: When targeting high-frequency trading strategies requiring sub-second execution
core.action: Claim or imply that ZVT supports sub-second trading execution — the minimum IntervalLevel is LEVEL_TICK (5-second minimum per tick), and the polling-based real_time mode in Trader.run() uses sleep-based waiting that cannot achieve sub-second responsiveness

consequence.description: Users build high-frequency strategies expecting sub-second latency execution, but the system cannot process ticks faster than 5-second intervals, causing missed trading opportunities and stale signal execution at sub-second timescales

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "Trader", "IntervalLevel" in when/action

core.when: When applying this framework to markets outside China (A-shares via eastmoney/joinquant/jointquant)
core.action: Claim or imply that ZVT supports live trading in non-Chinese markets — the framework is currently focused on China A-shares via eastmoney/jointquant/sina/em/joinquant providers; no international market data providers or broker integrations for US/HK/EU markets are implemented

consequence.description: Users attempt to deploy strategies on US or HK markets expecting the same data coverage and execution infrastructure, but no live data feeds or broker integrations exist for these markets, resulting in empty datasets and no order execution capability

_classification.rationale: A.3 FAILS: slash/module path detected in when/action

core.when: When using free data providers (em/eastmoney, joinquant, sina) for production or live trading decisions
core.action: Claim that free data providers are reliable or production-grade — these providers are explicitly described as unstable, potentially unavailable at any time, and requiring significant maintenance effort; they are suitable only for learning and research

consequence.description: Production trading systems built on free data providers experience unexpected data gaps, API changes, or complete unavailability during critical trading periods, causing strategy failures and financial losses from missing or stale market data

_classification.rationale: A.3 FAILS: slash/module path detected in when/action

core.when: When upgrading the zvt package to a newer version
core.action: Assume or promise backward compatibility between versions — the project explicitly does not guarantee any backward compatibility and reserves the right to change or discontinue any component without notice

consequence.description: Automated upgrades break existing strategies, data schemas, or API contracts without deprecation warnings, causing production systems to fail silently or raise exceptions on next run

_classification.rationale: A.6 FAILS: repo token "repo_token:zvt" in when/action

core.when: When configuring the TRADITIONAL Trader for backtesting with kdata_use_begin_time=False (the default)
core.action: Use end-of-period kdata prices for signal generation and order execution — with kdata_use_begin_time=False, the trading loop processes signals using the closed kdata's close price at the period end; this is the default and anti-lookahead-safe mode

consequence.description: Setting kdata_use_begin_time=True uses the open price of the current period for execution, which introduces look-ahead bias in backtesting since the open price is revealed only after the period begins, making backtest results unrealistically optimistic

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "Trader" in when/action

core.when: When executing TRADITIONAL Trader strategies across multiple timezone-aware data sources
core.action: Verify each timestamp comparisons and trading calendar checks use consistent timezone handling — Mixin.timestamp has no enforced timezone, and Trader.in_trading_date() compares to_date_time_str(timestamp) against the pre-loaded trading_dates list; mixing UTC and CST timestamps produces wrong trading date classifications

consequence.description: Stocks from different exchanges (US, HK, China) with incompatible timezone handling cause the trading calendar check to fail, resulting in signals being ignored on valid trading days or executed on non-trading days

_classification.rationale: A.1 FAILS: CamelCase "Trader", "Mixin" in when/action
